findings.description: MAX_PRO_CALLS_PER_SESSION=30 audit — VERIFIED REASONABLE: Matches daily_pro_limit=30 in async_client.py:151 (init_gateway). Worst-case usage: 5 hypotheses * 5 API calls = 25, plus 1 decision call = 26. Total: 26/30 = 87% utilization. Headroom of 4 calls for edge cases. Value is consistent and conservative.

No runtime validation that config values are internally consistent. If someone changes MAX_PRO_CALLS_PER_SESSION to 10 without adjusting MAX_HYPOTHESES_PER_SESSION=5 and MAX_API_CALLS_PER_THREAD=5, the pipeline could hit the cap mid-session. This is a configuration management concern, not a code vulnerability — config files are trusted input. Recommend adding an assert or startup validation that MAX_PRO_CALLS_PER_SESSION >= MAX_HYPOTHESES_PER_SESSION * MAX_API_CALLS_PER_THREAD.

All thresholds are conservative and well-documented: DIMINISHING_RETURNS_THRESHOLD=0.05 (5% gain floor), CONFIDENCE_ACTION_THRESHOLD=0.70 (high bar for recommendations), EXPECTATION_GAP_THRESHOLD=0.15 (15% gap for trade value). No values appear to enable risky behavior.
Verification layer weights (lines 22-25) sum to 1.00: 0.30 + 0.25 + 0.25 + 0.20 = 1.00. Correctly normalized. No bias toward any single layer.

notes: Pure configuration module — no executable code, no I/O, no external data. MAX_PRO_CALLS_PER_SESSION=30 is consistent with gateway defaults and provides reasonable headroom. Internal consistency validation at startup would be a nice-to-have but is not a security requirement since config is trusted input.
